mapping.a_element: singular values of the environment/projector matrix truncated in the SVD step

brief_justification: The only overlap is that both touch a 'spectrum', but the machinery is entirely different: paper A is a numerical-linear-algebra acceleration (QR-based subspace iteration replacing a large truncated SVD by a small one to speed up CTMRG tensor-network contraction on GPUs), whereas paper B is rigorous semiclassical microlocal analysis (a new pseudodifferential calculus plus complex scaling giving uniform resolvent estimates that prove a Weyl law for black-hole resonances). Truncating singular values for data compression does not correspond role-for-role to analytically counting resonances via a Weyl asymptotic, so no coherent transferable mechanism maps between them.
